versionNotes: Sprint 4 co-design — students help shape Sprint 4 through 4 aspects with individual reflection + partner discussion
title: Sprint 4 Co-Design
description: Help design Sprint 4. For each of 4 aspects, reflect individually, then discuss with a partner. Switch partners after Aspect 2.

questions.infoBlock:
Aspect 1: What Should We Be Able to Do?

Think beyond specific assignments. After 16 weeks in this course, what should every student be able to do or demonstrate about professional development and self-directed learning? Think about capabilities that would matter to an employer, to your career, to your ability to grow independently.

Instructions: Write your individual response first. You will share and discuss with a partner next.
questions.prompt: What should every student who completes this course be able to do or demonstrate? Name 2-3 specific capabilities — not topics covered, but things you can actually DO.
questions.placeholder: Think about what matters beyond this course — what capabilities would make you valuable?

questions.infoBlock:
Partner Discussion — Aspect 1

Turn to the person next to you. Share your response. Listen to theirs. Then discuss together:

  • Where do your answers overlap? Where do they differ?
  • Think about the entire class — would your answers serve everyone, or just people like you?
  • What capability would you add after hearing your partner?
questions.prompt: After discussing with your partner, write down: (1) your partner's name, (2) the capabilities you both agreed matter most, and (3) anything that changed in your thinking.
questions.placeholder:
Partner name: ...
Agreed capabilities: ...
What changed: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Aspect 2: What Activities Help Us Get There?

Given what you said we should learn in Aspect 1 — what types of activities would actually develop those abilities? Think about what has worked so far: self-assessments, triad accountability, information diet audits, reflections. What has not worked? Consider both in-class sessions and homework.

Instructions: Write your individual response. Same partner discussion follows.
questions.prompt: What types of activities — in class and as homework — would help develop the capabilities you identified? Name at least one activity type that has worked well this semester and one that has not.
questions.placeholder:
Activities that work: ...
Activities that don't: ...
What would be better: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Partner Discussion — Aspect 2

Share your response. Then discuss:

  • Which activities from Sprints 1-3 actually changed how you think or work?
  • Which felt like busywork?
  • If you could design one in-class activity and one homework activity for Sprint 4, what would they be?
questions.prompt: After discussing: (1) your partner's name (same partner), (2) activities you agreed work best, (3) what you would change or add for Sprint 4.
questions.placeholder:
Partner name: ...
Best activities: ...
Changes for Sprint 4: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Switch partners. Find a different person to sit with for Aspects 3 and 4.

Aspect 3: How Do We Know We Got There?

Sprint 4 ends with a demo and a portfolio defense where you prove your growth across the UMPIRE cycle. How should we assess whether you have actually developed the capabilities from Aspect 1? What counts as evidence? What format lets you show your growth most honestly?
questions.prompt: How should we assess your learning — what counts as real evidence of growth? And what defense format would let you show that growth most effectively? Consider: presentations, portfolio walkthroughs, peer challenges, Q&A sessions, or something else.
questions.placeholder:
What counts as evidence: ...
Best defense format: ...
Why: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Partner Discussion — Aspect 3

Share with your NEW partner. Discuss:

  • What kind of evidence would convince a skeptic that you actually grew?
  • Should the defense be 1-on-1 with the instructor, in small groups presenting each other's work, or something else?
  • Think about what would work for the whole class, not just you.
questions.prompt: After discussing with your NEW partner: (1) new partner's name, (2) what evidence format you agreed on, (3) what defense structure would work best for the class.
questions.placeholder:
New partner name: ...
Evidence format: ...
Defense structure: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Aspect 4: What Should Sprint 4 Look Like?

Sprint 4 is about preparing for and delivering your portfolio defense. The defense covers your entire journey across the UMPIRE cycle. Given everything you discussed in Aspects 1-3, what should Sprint 4 include to best prepare you?
questions.prompt: What should Sprint 4 look like? What weekly structure would help you prepare for the portfolio defense? What would make the final 4 weeks feel like they matter?
questions.placeholder:
Weekly structure: ...
What would make this meaningful: ...

questions.infoBlock:
Partner Discussion — Aspect 4

Share with same partner as Aspects 3-4. Discuss:

  • If you designed Sprint 4 for the whole class, what would the non-negotiables be?
  • What should be flexible?
  • One concrete recommendation to Prof. Sathya.
questions.prompt: Final discussion capture: (1) partner's name, (2) your group's top recommendation for Sprint 4, (3) one thing that should definitely stay and one thing that should definitely change.
questions.placeholder:
Partner name: ...
Top recommendation: ...
Keep: ...
Change: ...
